Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The following is a list of publicly traded companies, that have the largest market capitalization or "market value".[1] Most or all of these companies would be categorized as megacap stocks.

Market capitalization is calculated by multiplying the share price on a selected day and the number of outstanding shares on that day. The list is expressed in USD millions, using exchange rates from the selected day to convert other currencies.[2]

Trillion-dollar companies

[edit]

The table below lists all companies that have ever had a market capitalization of $1 trillion or more, their peak value, and the date on which their market cap first exceeded each milestone.[3][4]

Company Country Peak value (in billions USD) Peak value date When first passed Ref.
Nominal Inflation-
adjusted[5]		 $1 trillion $2 trillion $3 trillion $4 trillion $5 trillion
Nvidia 5,724 5,724 14 May 2026 30 May 2023 23 Feb 2024 5 Jun 2024 9 Jul 2025 29 Oct 2025 [6][7][8][9][10][11]

[12]

Apple 4,290 4,290 2 Dec 2025 2 Aug 2018 19 Aug 2020 3 Jan 2022 28 Oct 2025 — [13][14][15][16]
Alphabet 4,230 4,230 2 Feb 2026 16 Jan 2020 8 Nov 2021 15 Sep 2025 12 Jan 2026 — [17][18][19][20]
Microsoft 4,190 4,190 31 Jul 2025 25 Apr 2019 22 Jun 2021 24 Jan 2024 31 Jul 2025 — [21][22][23][24]
Amazon 2,910 2,910 3 Nov 2025 4 Sep 2018 26 Jun 2024 — — — [25][26][27]
Saudi Aramco 2,520 2,772 10 May 2022 12 Dec 2018 11 Dec 2019 — — — [28][29][30]
Meta 2,180 2,180 15 Aug 2025 28 Jun 2021 15 Aug 2025 — — — [note 1][31][32]
SpaceX 2,020 2,020 12 Jun 2026 12 Jun 2026 12 Jun 2026 — — — [33]
Broadcom 2,001 2,001 25 Apr 2026 13 Dec 2024 22 Apr 2026 — — — [34][35][36]
TSMC 1,821 1,821 15 Jan 2026 17 Oct 2024 24 Feb 2026 — — — [note 2][38][39][40]
Tesla 1,707 1,707 30 Jan 2026 25 Oct 2021 — — — — [41][42]
Berkshire Hathaway 1,192 1,192 3 May 2025 28 Aug 2024 — — — — [43][44]
Eli Lilly 1,167 1,167 5 Feb 2026 21 Nov 2025 — — — — [citation needed]
Walmart 1,075 1,054 5 Feb 2026 3 Feb 2026 — — — — [45]
Samsung 1,020 1,020 26 Feb 2026 26 Feb 2026 — — — — [citation needed]
PetroChina 1,200 1,744 5 Nov 2007 5 Nov 2007 — — — — [note 3]
Micron Technology 1,020 1,020 26 May 2026 26 May 2026 — — — — [49]
SK Hynix 1,080 1,080 27 May 2026 27 May 2026 — — — — [50][51][52]

Publicly traded companies

[edit]

Only companies with free float of at least 15% are included, and investment companies are excluded from these lists.[2] For lists based on Financial Times data, the value of unlisted stock classes is excluded.

Note: All market capitalization figures are in USD millions.

2026

[edit]

This list is up to date as of 31 March 2026[update]. Indicated changes in market value are relative to the previous quarter.

Rank First quarter

  • Forbes Global 2000
  • Most Valuable Nation Brands List

Notes

[edit]
  1. ↑ Meta was formerly Facebook Inc. and traded as Facebook, Inc. at the time it first reached a $1tn capitalization.
  2. ↑ On 8 July 2024, TSMC's American depository receipt (ADR) shares on the New York Stock Exchange briefly crossed the $1 trillion milestone due to the ratio of ADRs to ordinary shares listed in Taiwan.[37]
  3. ↑ On 5 November 2007, A shares of PetroChina ended their first day of trading after an IPO on the Shanghai Stock Exchange yielded a market cap of approximately $1 trillion.[46] Only 4 billion A shares were floated during the IPO, another 158 billion A shares were still held by China National Petroleum Corporation. 21.9 billion H shares were already floated on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.[47] However, market capitalization based on H shares never reached $500 billion.[48]
  4. 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 In case some companies with multiple classes of stock only one class is considered.

References

[edit]
  1. ↑ Grocer, Stephen (5 February 2019). "Biggest Public Company? Microsoft. Wait, Apple Again. Amazon? No, Back to Microsoft". The New York Times. ISSN 0362-4331. Archived from the original on 20 February 2022. Retrieved 20 February 2022.
  2. 1 2 "FT 500 2015 Introduction and methodology". Financial Times. 19 June 2015. Archived from the original on 2 September 2016. Retrieved 20 July 2015.
  3. 1 2 "Companies ranked by Market Cap - CompaniesMarketCap.com". companiesmarketcap.com. Archived from the original on 14 April 2021. Retrieved 9 December 2021.
  4. ↑ "Top companies in the world by market capitalization". Statista.
  5. ↑ Johnston, Louis; Williamson, Samuel H. (2023). "What Was the U.S. GDP Then?". MeasuringWorth. Retrieved 30 November 2023. United States Gross Domestic Product deflator figures follow the MeasuringWorth series.
  6. ↑ "Nvidia hits $1 trillion market cap". Yahoo! Finance. 30 May 2023. Archived from the original on 30 May 2023. Retrieved 30 May 2023.
  7. ↑ Saul, Derek (23 February 2024). "Nvidia Tops $2 Trillion Market Value For First Time Ever". Forbes. Archived from the original on 23 February 2024. Retrieved 23 February 2024.
  8. ↑ Leswing, Kif (5 June 2024). "Nvidia passes Apple in market cap as second-most valuable public U.S. company". CNBC. Archived from the original on 11 June 2024. Retrieved 11 June 2024.
  9. ↑ Subin, Samantha; Leswing, Kif (9 July 2025). "Nvidia briefly touched $4 trillion market cap for first time". CNBC. Retrieved 9 July 2025.
  10. ↑ Sethi, Vaamanaa (1 October 2025). "Nvidia share price hits new record high, becomes first company to top $4.5 trillion market cap | Stock Market News". mint. Retrieved 7 October 2025.
  11. ↑ Berkowitz, Ben. "Nvidia becomes the world's first $5 trillion company". Axios. Retrieved 29 October 2025.
  12. ↑ "Nvidia tops $5.5 trillion valuation as stock surges". Yahoo Finance. 14 May 2026. Retrieved 7 June 2026.
  13. ↑ Davies, Rob (2 August 2018). "Apple becomes world's first trillion-dollar company". TheGuardian.com. Archived from the original on 28 April 2020. Retrieved 3 November 2019.
  14. ↑ Popken, Ben (19 August 2020). "Apple just became the first U.S. Company worth $2 trillion". NBC News. Archived from the original on 19 August 2020. Retrieved 19 August 2020.
  15. ↑ Smith, Zachary Snowdon. "Apple Becomes 1st Company Worth $3 Trillion". Forbes. Archived from the original on 3 January 2022. Retrieved 3 January 2022.
  16. ↑ khan, Nauman (27 December 2024). "Record-Breaking: Apple Just $80B Away from $4 Trillion Market Cap". Yahoo Finance. GuruFocus. Retrieved 13 January 2025.
  17. ↑ Elias, Jennifer (16 January 2020). "Alphabet, Google's parent company, hits trillion-dollar market cap for first time". CNBC. Archived from the original on 30 October 2023. Retrieved 13 February 2024.
  18. ↑ Howley, Daniel (8 November 2021). "Google hits $2 trillion market cap, joining ranks of Microsoft and Apple". Yahoo! Finance. Archived from the original on 8 November 2021. Retrieved 8 November 2021.
  19. ↑ Vlastelica, Ryan (5 September 2025). "Alphabet Within Striking Range of $3 Trillion as Key Risk Clears". Bloomberg News. Archived from the original on 6 September 2025. Retrieved 6 September 2025 – via Yahoo News.
  20. ↑ Capelouto, J.D. (13 January 2026). "Alphabet Within Striking Range of $3 Trillion as Key Risk Clears". Semafor (website). Archived from the original on 13 January 2026. Retrieved 13 January 2026 – via Yahoo News.
  21. ↑ Levy, Ari (25 April 2019). "Microsoft hits $1 trillion market cap for the first time as stock jumps on earnings beat". CNBC. Archived from the original on 30 November 2023. Retrieved 13 February 2024.
  22. ↑ Vlastelica, Ryan; Bass, Dina (22 June 2021). "Microsoft Rises to Join Apple in Exclusive $2 Trillion Club". Bloomberg L.P. Archived from the original on 22 June 2021. Retrieved 22 June 2021.
  23. ↑ Oguh, Chibuike (24 January 2024). "Microsoft hits $3 trillion market value, second to Apple". Reuters. Retrieved 26 January 2024.
  24. ↑ Herrera, Sebastian. "Microsoft Just Became the World's Second $4 Trillion Company". The Wall Street Journal. Archived from the original on 31 July 2025. Retrieved 31 July 2025.
  25. ↑ DePillis, Lydia (4 September 2018). "Amazon is now worth $1,000,000,000,000". CNNMoney. Archived from the original on 31 January 2020. Retrieved 31 January 2020.
  26. ↑ Liu, Evie (20 December 2023). "Three Magnificent Seven Stocks Hit Record Highs This Past Month. Will the Others Follow?". Barron's. Archived from the original on 13 February 2024. Retrieved 13 February 2024.
  27. ↑ Oberoi, Mohit (5 February 2025). "Alphabet and Amazon.com Approach Mega Milestones". Barchart. Retrieved 6 September 2025 – via The Globe and Mail.{{cite web}}: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service (link)
  28. ↑ "Saudi Aramco becomes most valuable listed company in history". The Guardian. 11 December 2019. Archived from the original on 13 October 2023. Retrieved 10 December 2022.
  29. ↑ Riley, Charles (12 December 2019). "The world has its first $2 trillion company. But for how long?". CNN Business. Archived from the original on 12 December 2019. Retrieved 10 December 2022.
  30. ↑ Wael, Salma (10 May 2022). "Saudi Aramco briefly becomes world's top-valued company as shares surge". Arab News. Archived from the original on 13 February 2024. Retrieved 13 February 2024.
  31. ↑ Moon, Louise; Dodds, Io (28 June 2021). "Facebook joins $1 trillion club after antitrust win". The Daily Telegraph. Archived from the original on 13 February 2024. Retrieved 13 February 2024.
  32. ↑ Deffenbaugh, Ryan (12 August 2025). "IBD Stock Of The Day: Meta Nears New Milestone After Post-Earnings Rally". Investor's Business Daily. Retrieved 6 September 2025.{{cite news}}: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service (link)
  33. ↑ Harring, Alex (12 June 2026). "SpaceX market cap tops $2 trillion after shares of Elon Musk's rocket company gain 19% on debut". CNBC. Retrieved 12 June 2026.
  34. ↑ Bajwa, Arsheeya (13 December 2024). "Broadcom hits trillion-dollar valuation on lofty forecasts for AI demand". Reuters. Retrieved 14 December 2024.
  35. ↑ Kim, Tae; Clark, Adam (5 September 2025). "Broadcom Stock Soars to New High on CEO's Surprising AI Chip Outlook". Barron's. Retrieved 6 September 2025.
  36. ↑ "Broadcom just became the sixth company in U.S. history to achieve this feat". Morningstar, Inc. 22 April 2026. Retrieved 16 May 2026.
  37. ↑ Yu, Jeanny; Yang, Charlotte; Patnaik, Subrat (8 July 2024). "TSMC Hits $1 Trillion in Market Value on Relentless AI Rally". Bloomberg News.
  38. ↑ Carson, Ed (18 October 2024). "Taiwan Semiconductor, AI Chips". Investor's Business Daily.
  39. ↑ Grothaus, Michael (22 July 2025). "TSMC just hit a $1 trillion market cap. These 5 companies could be next". Fast Company. Archived from the original on 26 July 2025.
  40. ↑ "TSMC (TSM) Market Cap Surges Past $2 Trillion Amid AI Growth". GuruFocus. 25 February 2026. Retrieved 25 February 2026.
  41. ↑ "Tesla breaches $1 trillion market cap". Reuters. 25 October 2021. Archived from the original on 25 October 2021. Retrieved 25 October 2021.
  42. ↑ Saul, Derek (17 December 2024). "Tesla Stock Earns Another Analyst Upgrade As Elon Musk Celebrates 'Inevitable' New All-Time High". Forbes. Retrieved 19 December 2024.{{cite web}}: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service (link)
  43. ↑ Li, Yun (28 August 2024). "Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway hits $1 trillion market value, first U.S. company outside of tech to do so". CNBC. Archived from the original on 28 August 2024. Retrieved 28 August 2024.
  44. ↑ Bary, Andrew (17 March 2025). "Berkshire Hathaway Stock Hits New High. It's Way Ahead of the S&P 500 This Year". Barron's.

  46. ↑ Wearden, Graeme (6 November 2007). "PetroChina makes its debut as world's first trillion-dollar firm". the Guardian.
  47. ↑ "PetroChina opens at 48.6 yuan per share in Shanghai debut, up 191%". Archived from the original on 7 November 2007.
  48. ↑ "Apple market value hits $601B". Yahoo Finance. 10 April 2012.
